title: Import Plan (SP13 T-6)
description: Authoritative shape of the user-reviewable import-plan.md emitted by skills/infer-vault-structure/import-plan.sh. The on-disk format is markdown with a YAML frontmatter wrapper, inline per-candidate ```yaml blocks, a routing-table markdown table, and a refinements ```yaml block. T-7 (User review/edit gate) reassembles this wrapper from the markdown to validate user-edited plans round-trip; the schema is intentionally permissive on user-editable fields (proposed_path, type, metadata) so that an in-place edit does not break validation. Bumps on breaking shape changes; T-7 review-gate.sh checks schema_version before consuming.

schema_version.description: Schema version anchor for this import plan. Bump on breaking shape changes; T-7 validates this before consuming a user-edited plan.

input_propose_taxonomy_schema_version.description: Echoed schema_version of the propose-taxonomy-output.json this plan was generated from. T-6 import-plan.sh refuses to run if its input is not propose-taxonomy/1; this field records that contract for downstream verification.

generated_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp at which the plan was rendered.

header.description: Corpus stats header — top-of-file summary echoed from upstream T-4/T-5 outputs.

n_records.description: Total IR records in the source corpus (echoed from propose-taxonomy.n_records).

n_clusters.description: Cluster count from upstream T-4 (excludes the unclassified bucket).

n_passes.description: Number of LLM passes T-5 actually ran. Pass-3 fired when items_mapped_pct < threshold.

items_mapped_pct.description: Fraction of n_records mapped to a non-unclassified candidate after the final pass.

llm_mode.description: Whether T-5 ran against the live Anthropic Messages API or against the deterministic stub.

embedding_mode_input.description: Whether T-4 used the live Voyage embedding API or the deterministic stub. Echoed from propose-taxonomy.embedding_mode_input.

warnings.description: Non-fatal warnings echoed from T-5 (e.g., pass-3 trigger). Surfaced to user so a low-mapped run is not silent.

unclassified_callout.description: PROMINENT call-out at the top of the rendered markdown when the unclassified pile carries items. Silent skip (rendered as a blank line, no call-out) when count = 0. Per spec L199 + T-15 UX validation criterion.

present.description: True when count > 0; false when the unclassified pile is empty.

count.description: Number of items in the unclassified candidate's source_items[].

copy.description: User-facing copy rendered into the top call-out. Welcoming + options-first + 'no item silently dropped' reassurance per T-15 UX criterion. Empty string when present = false.

vault_tree.description: Proposed vault folder hierarchy. Top-level keys are cluster names (archetype-defined by the adopter); each maps to a list of sub-cluster or subfolder names. User can add/remove top-level folders or subfolders by editing this block; schema is archetype-agnostic so any reasonable cluster structure validates.

project_metadata_blocks.description: One entry per type=project candidate. Each renders as an inline ```yaml block under its H3 heading in the markdown body. User edits the YAML in place; T-7 reassembles this array by walking the H3 sections.

routing_table.description: Per-source-item routing decisions. Row count MUST equal header.n_records (every IR record routes to exactly one candidate). Renders as a markdown table in the body; user can edit cells in place to re-route an item.

source_path.description: Absolute path of the source IR record.

source_hash.description: Stable hash of the source content; round-trips through user edits.

candidate_id.description: ID of the candidate this item routes to (project p0001..p9999 or 'unclassified'). User can edit to re-route.

destination.description: Vault-relative destination path (echoes candidate.proposed_path; user can override for a single item without changing the whole candidate).

confidence.description: Echoed from candidate.confidence at generation time. T-7 does not recompute on user edit.

low_confidence.description: True when confidence < 0.5. Surfaced visibly per row in the rendered table so flagged items are not buried.

non_project_dispositions.description: One entry per non-project candidate (type in [reference, meeting, unclassified]). Same shape as project_metadata_blocks; renders under the 'Doesn’t fit any project — disposition' H2 section.

refinements.description: Pass-2 + optional pass-3 merge/split/promote/demote operations surfaced from T-5. NOT auto-applied — surfaced for user review at T-7 review gate. Renders as a single ```yaml block at the bottom of the markdown.

from.description: Source candidate id(s). String for single-source ops (split, demote); array for multi-source ops (merge). Renderer surfaces BOTH shapes faithfully.

into.description: Destination candidate id(s). String for single-target ops (merge, promote); array for multi-target ops (split). Renderer surfaces BOTH shapes faithfully.

candidate_block.description: One candidate's full record. Used inline as ```yaml under each H3 in the markdown body. User edits any field in place; T-7 round-trip preserves source_hash + candidate_id as stable anchors.

candidate_id.description: Stable candidate id from T-5. p0001..p9999 for typed candidates; the literal 'unclassified' for the terminal pile.

label.description: Short human-readable label. Used as the H3 heading. User can edit.

type.description: Candidate type. User can re-classify (e.g., promote a reference into a project) by editing this field; T-7 honors the edit when reassembling the wrapper.

proposed_path.description: Vault-relative path for the candidate. Empty string for unclassified. User can edit to re-place.

metadata.description: Free-form per-candidate metadata. T-8 (PRD/Context/Updates generator) consumes for project candidates.

source_items.description: IR records routed to this candidate. Echoed from propose-taxonomy candidate.source_items.

confidence.description: Heuristic dominant-origin-cluster fraction from T-5. Surfaced visibly; not hidden behind a fold.

low_confidence.description: True when confidence < 0.5. Renderer surfaces this as a visible flag in the H3 heading (e.g., '⚠️ low confidence') so flagged candidates are not buried.
